title: Connecting with a Fragmented Audience: Apple's iPhone 15 Launch
author: Oros Tovar, Edward Alexis
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The theoretical part of the article explains step by step how audience fragmentation is understood and why brands need an omnichannel strategy. First, it reviews important studies about consumer behavior and digital metrics. Then, it presents a simple framework that connects KPIs, content formats, and ways to measure results. After that, it describes the case study method used to see cause-effect relations, choose samples, and collect both qualitative and quantitative data. Finally, it summarizes the main implications and limits, explaining the data collection steps, thematic analysis, and triangulation to improve validity.  
  The case study explains the steps Apple followed for the iPhone 15 launch. First, the team chose the main platforms and created content adapted to each one. Then, they posted the material at the same time on TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and Twitter/X and checked the KPIs. After that, they studied interactions, views, and conversions, and changed the tactics in real time when results were low. They also compared the final numbers with the first goals. In the end, the study gives practical lessons, future research ideas, limits, and advice for small companies to use budgets better.
